prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `668eec9a`.
codex_response:
The rule is: take all non-background colors, order them by the topmost row where each color first appears, prepend the background color until there are five rows total, then emit a `5x3` grid of solid-color stripes. The summary hint was usable as a starting hypothesis and matched the official examples, so I did not add a `notes.md`.

I added a new task package at [arc2/tasks/task_668eec9a/__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_668eec9a/__init__.py), [arc2/tasks/task_668eec9a/verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_668eec9a/verifier.py), and [arc2/tasks/task_668eec9a/generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_668eec9a/generator.py). The generator stays close to the official family: fixed `16x16` orange background, sparse ordered diagonal traces, and an optional long bottom-row bar for the last color. No new primitive was needed in `arc2/dsl_ext.py`.

Validation passed end to end: `4/4` official examples, `1000/1000` generated examples, artifact build succeeded, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/668eec9a/previews/preview_01.png) against [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/668eec9a/previews/originals.png).
